description_long: This apartment building complex features two distinct sections: a set of twin high-rise towers with a modern, white facade and a lower, rectangular block with a brick exterior. The high-rise towers are characterized by their repetitive, vertically aligned balconies and flat roofs, while the brick section has a more traditional look with evenly spaced, rectangular windows across four stories. The complex is integrated with a road and sidewalk infrastructure, suggesting urban placement and accessibility. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the overall structure appears robust and functional, likely constructed from reinforced concrete, brick, and glass. The building's design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on practicality and efficient use of space.
description: A modern apartment complex with twin high-rise towers and a four-story brick block, featuring symmetrical windows and integrated road access.
description_view_0: Side view showing the four-story brick section and the narrow profile of the high-rise towers, with road access visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin high-rise towers with balconies and the entrance area connected to the road.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the brick section and the side of the high-rise towers, with road infrastructure visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the brick section's symmetrical window arrangement and the tops of the high-rise towers.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, grid-like pattern, with each floor featuring evenly spaced, rectangular windows aligned both vertically and horizontally.
